descript.abstract: World Gazetteer, 2022 is a point theme that represents the locations and proper names for map features around the world. The gazetteer includes attribute and annotation name information from various layers of the Digital Chart of the World.
descript.purpose: World Gazetteer provides a basemap layer that may be used to find locations by their proper name anywhere around the world. The categories include airports, coastal features, drainage features, islands, land features, ocean features, political features, and populated places.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing and consulting services to your local Esri Regional Office. For support, contact Esri by telephone (voice) between 5:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. Pacific time, Monday through Friday, by dialing +1-888-377-4575; facsimile (fax) available at +1-909-792-0960; or visit https://support.esri.com/; Esri holidays excluded. Email and chat are available through the Support page on My Esri (https://my.esri.com).
Outside the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing, sales, support, and consulting services to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0. For other questions or comments, you may contact Esri headquarters by email, telephone, or fax or write to us.

dataqual.logic: The dataset is converted to file geodatabase (FGDB) format using tools in ArcGIS. The geometry is checked, and if needed repaired.
dataqual.complete: After processing, the dataset is checked for drawing display and number of records and file sizes compared with source materials.

horizpa.horizpar: The horizontal accuracy, at a 90 percent confidence level for circular error, ranges from 1,600 feet to 7,300 feet. These figures are the root sum square of Operational Navigation Chart (ONC) and Jet Navigation Chart (JNC) sheet positional errors plus the additional error introduced by the Digital Chart of the World production process. Since the features are derived from annotation, they do not correspond with the true location because the latitude and longitude coordinate values were derived from the annotation location rather than the map feature itself. Locations taken from annotation are based on the lower left corner of the first letter. Also, the names associated with fuzzy polygons may occur anywhere in the general area of the feature. For example, the name 'Rocky Mountains' is a point located somewhere in the general area covered by the term Rocky Mountains. Fuzzy polygon names, which appeared on more than one ONC sheet, will occur more than once.

citeinfo.origin: U.S. Defense Mapping Agency, presently known as U.S. National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA)
citeinfo.title: Digital Chart of the World (DCW)

citeinfo.origin: United States Department of State, Bureau of Intelligence and Research
citeinfo.title: Geographic and Global Issues Quarterly

pubinfo.publish: United States Department of State, Bureau of Intelligence and Research

citeinfo.title: The World Factbook
citeinfo.othercit: The World Factbook was an annual publication, but now it is available online.

procstep.procdesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Extracted the features from the Digital Chart of the World. Restructured attributes for improved feature classifications. Updated attribute values based on political changes and other factors. Created ArcGIS layer file (.lyr). Created ArcView GIS legend file (.avl). Created projection file (.prj). Created spatial indices.

The following steps were performed by Esri: Updated FIPS_CNTRY attribute for East Timor and Serbia & Montenegro. Created ArcGIS layer file (.lyr), projection file (.prj), and spatial indices. Converted the dataset to SDC.
The following steps were performed by Esri: Updated attribute values for Taiwan. Removed the Paracel and Spratly island groups.
The following steps were performed by Esri: Reduced the attribute widths for CATEGORY and DESCRIPT.

The following steps were performed by Esri: Duplicated the point for the 'Sea of Japan' and renamed the new point 'East Sea'. Moved the 'Sea of Japan' point closer to Japan and moved the 'East Sea' point closer to the Korean peninsula. Changed name from 'Kiev' to 'Kyiv' for the four records related to Kyiv, Ukraine.

The following steps were performed by Esri: Updated the FIPS_CNTRY attribute for all points in Serbia to 'RB' and in Montenegro to 'MJ'. Converted the dataset to SDC. Created projection file (.prj), and spatial indices.

The following steps were performed by Esri: Used Data & Maps 2012 World Countries to select all gazetteer points that fell within South Sudan. Calculated the field WITHIN = 'South Sudan'. Calculated the field FIPS_CNTRY = 'SS'. Converted the dataset to SDC. Created ArcGIS layer file (.lyr), projection file (.prj), and spatial indices.
Converted the dataset into a file geodatabase.
The following steps were performed by Esri: Renamed the countries “Macedonia” to “North Macedonia” and “Swaziland” to “Eswatini”.
The following steps were performed by Esri: Renamed the country “Turkey” to “Turkiye”.

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

enttyp.enttypd: The points represent the locations and proper names for map features around the world.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The proper name of the feature.
attr.attrdefs: NGA, Esri; Esri; U.S. Department of State; U.S. Central Intelligence Agency; Government of Nunavut

attr.attrdef: The category to which the feature pertains.

edom.edomv: Airport, Coastal feature, Drainage feature, Island, Land feature, Ocean feature, Political, and Populated place

Administered by another country, Administrative Region, Administrative Subdivision, Area, Atoll, Autonomous Community, Autonomous Municipality, Autonomous Region, Bay, Canal, Canton, Cape, Capital City, Capital Municipality, Capital Territory, Channel, City, Commonwealth, Constitutional Province, Continent, Controlled and administered, County, Crown dependency, Dam, Department, Dependent territory, Desert, Disputed territory, District, Division, Economic Prefecture, Emirate, Fall, Federal Dependencies, Federal District, Federal Status City, Federal Territory, Fjord, Freely associated area, General area, Glacier, Governorate, Gulf, Head, Hill or Hills, Independent state, Independent Town, Inland island, Inland island group, Intendancy, International, Isthmus, Lagoon, Lake, Landing, Military, Mountain, Mountains, Municipal, Municipality, National District, National Territory, Ocean, Ocean hazard, Oceanic island, Oceanic island group, Other, Other airport, Other mountains, Other ocean feature, Overseas administrative division, Overseas department, Overseas territory, Pakistan-administered, Parish, Peninsula, Point, Populated place, Prefecture, Province, Region, Republic, Reservoir, Ria, River, River mouth, Salt flat, Sea, Seasonal river, Self-governing administrative division, Self-governing commonwealth, Self-governing territory, Self-governing (freely associated), Special City, Special District, Special Municipality, Special Region, State, Strait, Territorial collectivity, Territory, To be determined, Topographic feature, Town, Unincorporated territory, Union Territory, Volcano, Well or Spring, Wetlands, and Zone

attr.attrdef: The name for the geographic entity (usually a country) in which the feature is located.
attr.attrdefs: NGA, Esri; Esri; U.S. Department of State; U.S. Central Intelligence Agency

edom.edomv: Named Countries, Continents, Oceans, Seas, and Gulfs
edom.edomvd: The list of types of WITHIN values.
edom.edomvds: NGA, Esri; Esri; U.S. Department of State; U.S. Central Intelligence Agency

attr.attrdef: The code (two-letter) for the country in which the feature is located.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States, contact the Esri Sales staff at +1-800-447-9778 or visit https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=1 for more information about our software and data.
Outside the United States, direct all inquiries to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0.

distinfo.distliab: See access and use constraints information.

digtinfo.formcont: To use this data requires software that works with FGDB files.

stdorder.ordering: Data and Maps is available only as part of ArcGIS software.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
